tasks.name: Empirical scan: Arabic back-reference patterns in fixtures (Defense 1A)
tasks.prompt:
Run the empirical back-reference scanner:
  python scripts/empirical_backrefs.py --output overnight/results/empirical-backrefs/scan.json

Read the output and write a brief interpretation to overnight/results/empirical-backrefs/summary.md:
- Which patterns had the most hits?
- Is the hit rate sufficient to justify building Defense 1A?
- What is the recommended decision (PROCEED or SKIP)?

tasks.name: Knowledge integrity: attribution corruption probe (excerpting)
tasks.prompt:
KNOWLEDGE CORRUPTION PROBE — T-1: Attribution Corruption

Read engines/excerpting/src/phase3_deterministic.py, focusing on _compute_layer_coverages() and compute_layer_attribution().

Hunt for scenarios where layer attribution produces WRONG RESULTS:
1. All layers have author_canonical_id=None — does merging collapse distinct layers?
2. Two layers with same (type, author) but different scholarly functions
3. LA rule cascade with exact boundary values (80.0%, 100.0%)
4. compute_quoted_scholars excluding correct layers or including wrong ones

For EACH scenario found, write a targeted test with real Arabic text.
If you find a bug, FIX IT and explain why it corrupts knowledge.
Run: python -m pytest engines/excerpting/tests/ -x -v --tb=short

tasks.name: Codex review: Knowledge integrity: attribution corruption probe (excerpting)
tasks.prompt: Review the code changes from task 'Knowledge integrity: attribution corruption probe (excerpting)'. Check: type safety, Pydantic patterns, test completeness, error handling. Do NOT evaluate Arabic text or domain logic. Run: git diff HEAD~1

tasks.name: Knowledge integrity: Arabic text round-trip probe (excerpting)
tasks.prompt:
KNOWLEDGE CORRUPTION PROBE — T-3: Text Corruption

Read engines/excerpting/src/writer.py.

Verify Arabic text survives serialization BYTE-FOR-BYTE:
1. Text with full tashkeel (diacritics) — fathah, dammah, kasrah, shadda, sukun
2. ZWNJ (U+200C) between Arabic letters
3. Tatweel (U+0640) inside words
4. Superscript alef (U+0670)
5. Paragraph breaks (\n\n) in primary_text

Write to JSONL, read back, verify byte-identical.
If Pydantic or JSON serialization alters any byte, this is CRITICAL.
Run: python -m pytest engines/excerpting/tests/ -x -v --tb=short

tasks.name: Codex review: Knowledge integrity: Arabic text round-trip probe (excerpting)
tasks.prompt: Review the code changes from task 'Knowledge integrity: Arabic text round-trip probe (excerpting)'. Check: type safety, Pydantic patterns, test completeness, error handling. Do NOT evaluate Arabic text or domain logic. Run: git diff HEAD~1

tasks.name: Review recently modified excerpting code (4 files)
tasks.prompt:
TASK: Code review of recently modified excerpting engine source files.

Read engines/excerpting/CLAUDE.md for context.
Read the SPEC section governing these files.

Files modified in the last 24 hours:
  - engines/excerpting/src/phase2_classify.py
  - engines/excerpting/src/phase2_group.py
  - engines/excerpting/src/phase3_consensus.py
  - engines/excerpting/src/phase3_enrichment.py

Review for:
1. SPEC compliance — does the code match the behavioral rules?
2. Arabic text safety — no .lower()/.upper()/.strip() on Arabic, no \d in regex
3. D-023 metadata pass-through — all upstream fields preserved
4. Error handling — fails loudly with SPEC error codes?
5. Edge cases that lack test coverage

Write detailed findings to overnight/results/review-recent-excerpting/review.md
Do NOT modify any source files. This is a READ-ONLY review.

tasks.name: Edge case hardening for recent excerpting changes
tasks.prompt:
TASK: Write edge case and hardening tests for recently modified excerpting code.

Read engines/excerpting/CLAUDE.md for context.
Read the review at overnight/results/review-recent-excerpting/review.md for findings.
Read existing tests in engines/excerpting/tests/.

Recently modified files:
  - engines/excerpting/src/phase2_classify.py
  - engines/excerpting/src/phase2_group.py
  - engines/excerpting/src/phase3_consensus.py
  - engines/excerpting/src/phase3_enrichment.py

For each finding in the review (especially HIGH and MEDIUM):
1. Write a targeted test that exercises the edge case
2. Use real Arabic text from tests/fixtures/ wherever possible
3. Follow conftest factory patterns

Rules: use [0-9] not \d. Never .lower()/.upper()/.strip() on Arabic.
Run: python -m pytest engines/excerpting/tests/ -x -v --tb=short
All existing + new tests must pass.

tasks.name: Codex review: Edge case hardening for recent excerpting changes
tasks.prompt: Review the code changes from task 'Edge case hardening for recent excerpting changes'. Check: type safety, Pydantic patterns, test completeness, error handling. Do NOT evaluate Arabic text or domain logic. Run: git diff HEAD~1

tasks.name: Cross-engine contract and D-023 metadata flow verification
tasks.prompt:
Run the metadata flow verification:
  python3 scripts/verify_metadata_flow.py
Also check cross-engine contracts:
  PYTHONIOENCODING=utf-8 python tools/check_cross_engine_contracts.py
Report findings to overnight/results/val-contracts/boundaries.md

tasks.name: Analyze test coverage gaps in excerpting engine
tasks.prompt: Read engines/excerpting/SPEC.md section 4 and extract every behavioral rule (numbered items, MUST/SHALL statements). Then read all test files in engines/excerpting/tests/ and match each SPEC rule to its test(s). Write a coverage matrix to overnight/results/test-coverage-excerpting/coverage.md showing which rules have tests and which lack tests. Current test count: 739.

tasks.name: Test suite regression check
tasks.prompt:
Run the full test suite for all completed engines SEPARATELY:
  python -m pytest engines/source/tests/ -v --tb=short
  python -m pytest engines/normalization/tests/ -v --tb=short
  python -m pytest engines/excerpting/tests/ -v --tb=short
Report: total tests, passed, failed, skipped, any error details.
Write report to overnight/results/val-test-regression/summary.md
Do NOT modify any files.
